greenfieldRuntimeProof.claim: Greenfield app-build route produces approved build contract and executable plan with emitted app tree

dirtyRepoRuntimeProof.claim: Dirty-repo intake/audit/repair flow with completion contract and launch packet

selfUpgradeRuntimeProof.claim: Self-upgrade spec, drift detection, regression guard with validator enforcement

universalPipelineRuntimeProof.claim: Universal capability pipeline with full multi-artifact contract (truth, specs, plans, code, validation, launch packet)

multiDomainEmittedOutputProof.representativeMeasure: provided across required launch domains (web_saas_app, marketing_website, api_service, mobile_app, bot, ai_agent, game, dirty_repo_completion)

domainRuntimeCommandExecutionProof.claim: Per-domain command matrix, machine-readable logs, explicit execution skips, fail-closed validator

domainRuntimeDepthMatrix.claim: Launch rubrics and runtime depth across supported domain builders

runtimeCommandProofSummary.claim: Runnable commands are local-runtime readiness evidence for representative emitted trees, not exhaustive production deployment assertion

externalDeploymentReadinessSummary.claim: External integration contracts, secret hygiene, deployment instruction presence validated per domain
externalDeploymentReadinessSummary.caveat: Does not perform live calls to external services (Vercel, Auth0, Stripe, Supabase, etc.); not a substitute for production smoke testing

deploymentDryRunSummary.claim: Deployment config validation, preview artifact generation, smoke-test plans, rollback plans per domain
deploymentDryRunSummary.caveat: Does not perform live deployment, use real credentials, or call external production APIs

credentialedDeploymentReadinessSummary.claim: Credential requirement manifests, approval-gate blocked-by-default, provider adapters, secret policy checks
credentialedDeploymentReadinessSummary.caveat: Declares what credentials and approvals are required; does not validate real credentials or execute live deployment

liveDeploymentExecutionReadinessSummary.claim: Deployment-attempt contract, approval request, credential binding, provider execution adapter, pre-deploy checklist, blocked execution plan, planned smoke/rollback, audit trail
liveDeploymentExecutionReadinessSummary.caveat: Demonstrates approval-gated and credential-gated execution orchestration only; does not execute live deployment, does not call real provider APIs, does not validate real credentials, does not prove production deployment success

secretsCredentialManagementReadinessSumm.claim: Metadata-only secret references, provider credential profiles, redacted audit events, and deployment preflight secret gating are enforced
secretsCredentialManagementReadinessSumm.caveat: Secret values are never persisted in repository evidence; only secret references and fingerprints are recorded, and live deployment remains blocked when required secrets are missing

explicitCaveats: Emitted-output proof is representative across required launch domains, not exhaustive over every blueprint permutation, environment-specific deployment path, or external integration surface.
Runnable command proof is local-runtime readiness evidence for representative emitted trees; does not assert exhaustive production deployment success across all infrastructures or integrations.
External integration/deployment proof validates integration contracts, secret hygiene, and deployment instruction presence; does not perform live calls to external services.
Deployment dry-run proof validates deployment configuration, preview generation, and plan generation; does not perform live deployment or use real credentials.
Credentialed deployment readiness proof declares what credentials and approvals are required; does not validate real credentials or execute live deployment.
Live deployment execution readiness proof demonstrates approval-gated and credential-gated execution orchestration only; does not execute live deployment or validate real credentials.
Secrets and credentials are represented by metadata-only references and fingerprints; plaintext secret values are never committed to repository evidence.
No exhaustive proof is provided over every possible blueprint/domain/provider/integration permutation. Proof is representative of core launch-critical surfaces.
